channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Cargo bike blocking EV charging point, 28th March
item.description:
This cargo bike belongs to 80 Glenarm Road. They chain it to the lamppost outside their house. It blocks the EV charging point and it could damage any cars. This has been reported before. I am unable to charge my car when this bike is here.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glenarm Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish on the street, 28th March
item.description:
We have had rubbish dumped on our street for the last 6 months. Someone has been dumping clothes, garden waste and furniture. Can you please put a warning sign to inform this is not a dumping site.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Primrose Square, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing cardboard rubbish, 28th March
item.description:
I work at Stoke Newington Fire Station and we got called here for an incident and noticed significant fire loading outside this property.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Osbaldeston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: E-bike on pavement, 28th March
item.description:
E-bike obstructing the footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cracked pavement slab, 28th March
item.description:
Cracked slab, lifted on one end which could be a trip hazard or cause if fallen upon.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sandhurst Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped, 28th March
item.description:
Rubbish has been dumped to the side of my house. This keeps happening and make the street l, our house look so untidy.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): East Bank, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole, 28th March
item.description:
Hi This pothole is uneven and could cause damage. in thd junction near 34 Priestlands park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bin, 28th March
item.description:
Someone dumped this bin on the corner of the street mid Jan. We’ve had to move it so it is safe for people to use the pavement but it is never emptied. It doesn’t have a lid (used to be a blue bin). We store our bins in a private area so it’s not ours. If you could remove we’d be very grateful

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookhill Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neighbor is hoarding waste clutter on public areas, 28th March
item.description:
Number 42 Alberta Street is continuing to accumulate waste and clutter on public path and garden despite being asked to remove it by severel neighbors.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alberta Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole Crescent, 28th March
item.description:
Please repair these 2 potholes on the side of the road that may cause vehicle damage. They are in the cirner of 34 Priestlands Park and The Crescent.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Road, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Radiator, 28th March
item.description:
Damp from few days a week a radiator

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Marcon Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicles double parking and on double yellow lines, 28th March
item.description:
Several vehicles are being dumped on the whole length of Rollins Street as the parking restrictions are not being enforced. Vehicles are dumped for up to six months at a time sometimes longer. One bin lorry has been dumped for several years now. This issue is causing serious congestion and causing traffic to back up. It is hazardous as the way the abandoned vehicles are dumped your view is obscured and you can’t see on coming vehicles which is extremely dangerous. At times two cars cannot drive down the road at the same time. The road is narrow under the bridge and because cars are not being ticketed on the yellow lines cars are parked on both sides. One vehicle currently has a notice from the London Fire Brigade stating that they could not attend an incident as the abandoned car had restricted access, very worrying and seeing that makes me and my neighbours feel very unsafe in our homes. This is extremely serious and dangerous. Should there be a fire or someone needs an ambulance our lives are being put at risk as the emergency services cannot get through. Everyone knows in such instances a few minutes could be the difference between life and death. I myself could not take delivery of my fridge freezer and cooker as the delivery van could not get through due to an abandoned car restricting the width of the road. This whole situation is dangerous, worrying and frustrating. Not to mention how unsightly the street looks. Lewisham has failed to enforce parking restrictions or monitor the issue. Rollins Street is supposed to be a controlled parking zone but the job has been half done leaving the residents to deal with this anti social behaviour. I have so many more pictures but am only able to upload three. The whole length of Rollins Street is littered with abandoned vehicles. This whole issue has been ongoing for several years and has gotten progressively worse.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rollins Street, New Cross Gate, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree root damage, 28th March
item.description:
The root of the tree outside 87 Glenarm Road has caused damage to the pavement. The tarmac is raised and split someone could trip over it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glenarm Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lidl Trolley, 28th March
item.description:
Lidl trolley left in street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pound Park Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter spilling from skip on driveway of house, 28th March
item.description:
There is a house extension/ refurbishment underway near junction of A20 and Chapel Farm Rd. The skip is piled high with rubbish which is now blowing all over Chapel Farm Rd. It's really unpleasant.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chapel Farm Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped beds and mattresses need removal, 28th March
item.description:
Derby Road west side near junction with Victoria Park Road Flytipped beds and mattresses need removal

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Box of rubbish left on pavement. Box clearly shows the persons name and address. I have a photograph if you want his details., 28th March
item.description:
Rubbish is continually dumped in this location.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ruskin Drive, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in front of 1a Lynton Road, 28th March
item.description:
Two pallets and plastic crate left on the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lynton Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain cover has broken, 28th March
item.description:
Drain cover broken on corner of choumert road and bellenden road on even numbered side of road opp begging bowl restaurant.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 112, Peckham Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple gas canisters dumped along Court Yard, 28th March
item.description:
Appears to be a dumping site with several piles of rubbish nearest fence by parking spaces. All nitrous oxide canisters so dangerous fire hazard. It's been a few weeks so pile is being added to. Neighbours worried as there previously was a fire here last year!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chaundrye Close, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet
